date_notes: Five Canadian provinces raised minimum wages October 1 2025, leaving Alberta's $15/hr rate the lowest in Canada. Alberta's rate unchanged since October 2018.
title_en: Alberta minimum wage becomes lowest in Canada after seven years frozen; NDP tables bill to increase it
title_fr: Le salaire minimum de l'Alberta devient le plus bas au Canada après sept ans de gel ; le NPD dépose un projet de loi pour l'augmenter
description_en: Following minimum wage increases in Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Nova Scotia, and Prince Edward Island on October 1 2025, Alberta's $15/hour minimum wage — unchanged since the NDP raised it in October 2018 — becomes the lowest in Canada. The UCP government has not raised the minimum wage in over seven years in office. NDP MLA Kathleen Ganley tables Bill 201 proposing $1 annual increases reaching $18 by October 2027. Alberta's youth minimum wage remains $13/hour for workers under 18.

update_log.change: Sourced from R-086 (Service Canada Tier 1) and R-087 (CBC Oct 30 2025). EVT-052 is the 2019 youth minimum wage reduction.
